title: Identification Type
text: Identification Type The method used to identify the beginning of a subfield. Value Description None Variable length techniques not used. String Identifier Use an ASCII string to identify variable length fields. You can specify the respective strings using the Field Identifier and First Subfield Identifier . x -byte binary length Binary length where x is a value from one through four. String Id. and x -byte binary length Use a combination of string identifiers and binary length. x -byte binary length and String-Id Use a combination of binary length and the string identifiers. Description You can choose to use string identification, length identification, or a combination of the two. Choose the option that you want to use from the list provided. If the field does not include subfields, leave the subfield entries empty. If the field is fixed length, leave all these entries empty. Applies to Modeled Field Interface Related Links Variable-Length Fields Variable-length fields store only the actual data in the field, and not the trailing spaces that would be added for a fixed-length field. Field Identifier ASCII string that marks a variable-length field. First Subfield Identifier ASCII string that marks the first subfield when using variable-length techniques. Was this topic helpful?
